---
description: "Use when editing this Astro portfolio site, especially the homepage, section content, glassmorphism styling, mobile-first layout, and static assets."
applyTo: "src/**, public/**"
---

# Portfolio Website Instructions

- Keep the site as one long scrollable Astro page with sticky top navigation that jumps to sections.
- Preserve the user’s wording and voice. Do not rewrite content to sound polished or generic.
- When details, links, images, video, or documents are missing, leave clear placeholders such as `[description missing, add about 50 words here]`, `[project links missing]`, or `[Image missing]`.
- Optimize for mobile first with readable spacing, relative sizing, and layouts that scale across phones without hardcoded pixel-heavy positioning.
- Keep the visual style white and airy with frosted-glass cards, soft spacing, and accents from `#fae3f6`, white, blue, `#E723C1`, and `#6F23E7`.
- Avoid busy motion. No typing effects, particle backgrounds, or decorative animation systems. Use only subtle hover states.
- Keep the ornithopter as a visual motif: a thin translucent silhouette near the hero and a muted looping video in the timeline section when the asset exists.
- Follow the section order from the plan: Hero, Timeline, About, Research, Leadership, Projects, Awards, Skills, Learning, Hobbies, Contact.
- Use Astro static-site patterns that work cleanly on GitHub Pages.
- Prefer clear, readable section headings, large cards for featured content, and short blocks of text over dense layouts.
